subject: Reconstruction artaefacts in tomograms reconstructed from half-sets
body:
Hi all,

I am getting these grid-like reconstruction artaefacts, which I assume are 
associated with chunk-by-chunk reconstruction, in the tomograms 
reconstructed by Warp from odd/even frames (attachment 1).

Strangely, this seems to be the case only for tomograms reconstructed from 
half-sets; reconstructions from full set of frames seems to not have these 
artaefacts (attachment 2).

As you might have guessed, I am trying to denoise my tomograms in Warp 
using odd/even frame sets, but the denoiser appears to be working 
suboptimally because of these artaefacts. I tried a couple different 
binning factors for tomogram reconstruction, but the artaefacts are always 
there.

Has anyone experienced, and more importantly troubleshot this, before? I 
imagine increasing the "padding" while keeping the cropped volume the same 
for chunks might  get rid of these artaefacts, but I don't know how I can 
change the number/size/padding of chunks for tomogram reconstruction in 
warp. 

Also, why do these artaefacts not appear for tomograms reconstructed with 
full set of frames? Does warp use different padding for chunks for 
tomograms reconstructed from full-sets or half-sets?

I would appreciate any help with this.
Thanks,
Arjun
